stem: A binary search is being used to find the value 13 in the sorted list [1, 4, 7, 9, 11, 13, 17, 21, 25].
model_answer: Step 1: take the middle element of the list (index 4 → 11). Compare with 13: 13 > 11, so search the right half (indices 5..8 → [13, 17, 21, 25]). Step 2: take the middle of [13, 17, 21, 25] (index 6 → 17 in the original list). Compare: 13 < 17, so search the left half ([13]). Step 3: middle is 13, which matches. Found in 3 comparisons. Binary search relies on the list being sorted.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Look for: list must be sorted, middle element compared, halve based on comparison, repeat until found or empty. Accept variations in indexing convention.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe, with numbered steps, how a binary search finds the value 13 in the list above. Use the values from the list to illustrate each step.

mark_points.text: states that the list must be sorted (precondition)
compares the target with the middle element on each step
if target is greater than the middle, discards the lower half (and vice versa)
uses the values to reach 13 in 3 (or fewer) comparisons

misconceptions.label: Binary search on unsorted list
misconceptions.description: Binary search assumes a sorted list. Applying it to unsorted data can return 'not found' for an element that is present.
